title: Exceptions should not be thrown from property getters
description: Exceptions should not be thrown from property gettersAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellerror-handlingWhy is this an issue?Property getters should be simple operations that are always safe to call. If exceptions need to be thrown, it is best to convert the property to a method.It is valid to throw exceptions from indexed property getters and from property setters, which are not detected by this rule.Noncompliant code examplepublic int Foo { get { throw new Exception(); // Noncompliant } }Compliant solutionpublic int Foo { get { return 42; } }ExceptionsNo issue is raised when the thrown exception derives from or is of typeNotImplementedException,NotSupportedExceptionorInvalidOperationException.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your G
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Property getters should not throw exceptions - convert to a method instead

logic.reasoning: Detects property getters that throw exceptions other than the allowed types (NotImplementedException, NotSupportedException, InvalidOperationException). Property getters should be simple, side-effect-free operations. The pattern looks for 'get { ... throw new Exception' within ClassDeclaration nodes, using negative lookahead to exclude allowed exception types. Excludes property setters, indexed properties, comments, and tests.

should_trigger: public int Foo { get { throw new Exception(); } }
public string Name { get { throw new ArgumentException(); } }
public int Value { get { if (condition) throw new ApplicationException(); return 0; } }
should_not_trigger: public int Bar { get { throw new NotImplementedException(); } }
public string Name { set { throw new Exception(); } }
public int this[int index] { get { throw new Exception(); } }
// property getter: get { throw new Exception(); }

logic.self_critique_notes: Changed from PropertyDeclaration to ClassDeclaration since scanner has limited property detection. Pattern uses negative lookahead to exclude NotImplementedException, NotSupportedException, and InvalidOperationException which are allowed per the rule description. Added exclusions for property setters and indexed properties. Edge cases: (1) Indexed properties should not trigger - excluded via 'this[' pattern; (2) Property setters can throw - pattern checks specifically for 'get{' context; (3) Allowed exceptions - handled by negative lookahead.
